1.11a: Contact the office issuing Mortgage Instructions.
1.11b: Contact the office issuing Mortgage Instructions.
1.7: Lending is not permitted where the borrower and mortgagor are different entities.
1.14: Legal firm may not act if the person handling the transaction or their immediate family is the seller.
1.15: Legal firm may not act if the person handling the transaction or their immediate family is the borrower.
3.1.3: Notify the lender of the seller's legal representative's details only if specifically requested.
3.1.4: If the seller is unrepresented, contact the office issuing Mortgage Instructions.
3.1.5: Acceptable identity documents List A: Valid passport with English translation, UK full or provisional photocard driving license, old style UK paper driving license, Benefit book or letter (under 6 months old), Firearms or shotgun certificate, HMRC tax notification (under 6 months old, excluding SA302/P60/P45), NI Electoral ID card, EU/EEA photocard ID, non-EEA National ID card.

Acceptable identity documents List B: Electoral register search confirmation, utility bill (under 6 months old, excluding mobile), Bank/Building Society/Credit Union statement or passbook (under 6 months old, excluding internet statements), Mortgage statement (under 6 months old), Local Authority tax bill, Council tenancy agreement, HMRC tax notification (under 6 months old).
3.2.3: Notify the lender of the seller's legal representative's details only if specifically requested.
3.2.4: If the seller is unrepresented, contact the office issuing Mortgage Instructions.
4.5a: Contact the office issuing Mortgage Instructions for re-inspections.
4.5b: Send the certificate of title to the office issuing Mortgage Instructions.
4.1: Valuation report is included in the Mortgage Instructions unless specified otherwise.
4.3: If valuation assumptions are incorrect, contact the office issuing Mortgage Instructions.
5.1.1: Contact the office issuing Mortgage Instructions if the seller has owned the property for less than 6 months.
Lending is prohibited on back-to-back sales, sub-sales, or assigned contracts.
5.4.4: Environmental reports only required if requested by the valuer.
Report environmental concerns to the office issuing Mortgage Instructions.
5.4.5: Personal searches are only accepted if performed by firms regulated under The Search Code and monitored by the Property Codes Compliance Board.
5.4.6: Search insurance is accepted for remortgages only.
Search insurance is not accepted for property purchases.
5.5.3a: Report planning/building regulation breaches to the office issuing Mortgage Instructions.
5.5.3b: Original or copy of planning permission must be placed with the deeds.
5.5.3c: Original or copy of building regulation consents must be placed with the deeds.
5.5.3d: Certificates of lawful use or development must be placed with the deeds.
5.5.4: Report property restrictions affecting value to the office issuing Mortgage Instructions.
5.7.1a: Flying freeholds are acceptable if the affected area is 15% or less of the property.
5.7.1c: Report flying freeholds to the office issuing Mortgage Instructions.
5.8.1: Security comprising a building converted into max 4 flats is acceptable if the borrower occupies one and owns or shares the freehold.
5.10.1: Report restrictions on use to the office issuing Mortgage Instructions.
5.13.1: Contact the office issuing Mortgage Instructions if funds are not from the borrower or a second charge is proposed.
5.14.15a: Report absentee or insolvent landlords to the office issuing Mortgage Instructions.
5.14.15b: Indemnity insurance is accepted for absent or insolvent landlords.
5.14.1: Minimum unexpired lease term: 85 years from completion.
Help to Buy Shared Equity lease requirements: 250 years for houses, 125 years for flats.
Help to Buy ground rent must be reasonable and linked to RPI or similar index.
5.14.9: Ground rent must not exceed 0.1% of market value (up to 0.2% may be accepted subject to review).
Doubling ground rent clauses (e.g., every 5-20 years) are prohibited.
Report ground rent provisions that may create an Assured Shorthold Tenancy (rent >£250 or >£1,000 in London) unless the lease is varied or indemnity insurance is in place.
5.14.10: Contact the office issuing Mortgage Instructions for service charge matters.
5.14.11: Indemnity insurance is not accepted for unsatisfactory lease terms.
5.14.12: Place clear ground rent and service charge receipts with the deeds.
5.14.13: Place receipted notice or evidence of service with the deeds.
5.15.2a: Report management company issues to the office issuing Mortgage Instructions.
5.15.2b: Send management company share certificate to the lender.
5.15.2c: Send signed blank stock transfer form to the lender.
5.15.2d: Send management company memorandum and articles of association to the lender.
5.16.2: Contact the office issuing Mortgage Instructions regarding search entry issues.
5.17.5a: Send the power of attorney to the lender.
5.17.5b: Send the statutory declaration of non-revocation for power of attorney to the lender.
5.19.1: Lending on affordable housing, shared equity, or shared ownership is not permitted.
5.20.1: Report solar panel leases that do not meet UK Finance minimum requirements.
The lender must have an unconditional right to break the solar lease upon possession.
5.20.3: Lender requires an unconditional right to break solar PV leases in the event of possession.
5.20.4: Existing Green Deal Plans must be disclosed.
6.1.3: Notify the office issuing Mortgage Instructions if the borrower does not proceed.
6.2.1: Report property description discrepancies to the office issuing Mortgage Instructions.
6.3.1: Report purchase price issues to the office issuing Mortgage Instructions.
6.4.4: Report incentives at the earliest opportunity; reporting via Certificate of Title may delay completion.
6.4.5: Contact the office issuing Mortgage Instructions if purchase funds are not controlled by the legal firm.
6.5.1: Contact the office issuing Mortgage Instructions if vacant possession is not provided.
6.6.1: Contact the office issuing Mortgage Instructions regarding Buy-to-Let or existing tenancies.
6.6.2: Contact the office issuing Mortgage Instructions if letting details are missing at completion.
6.6.3: Send counterpart or certified copy of the tenancy agreement to the lender.
6.6.4: HMOs are not accepted as security.
6.7.1: Acceptable warranty schemes: NHBC, Zurich Municipal, Premier Guarantee, Building Life Plans, LABC Hallmark, Castle 10 (Checkmate), FMB Latent Defects, Build Zone, Q Assure Build, ICW, HomeProof New Build 10, One Guarantee, Advantage Home Construction Insurance (ACHI Limited), Ark Residential.
6.7.2: Place warranty certificates with the deeds.
6.7.3: Place assignments of building standards indemnity schemes with the deeds.
6.7.4: Contact the office issuing Mortgage Instructions if a new home lacks a warranty.
6.7.6: Place accepted professional consultant's certificates with the deeds.
6.8.1: Contact the office issuing Mortgage Instructions if no agreement or bond exists for unadopted roads/sewers.
6.9.1: Report missing easements to the office issuing Mortgage Instructions.
6.10.2: Retentions or instalments are released to the borrower unless the offer states otherwise.
6.11.1: Report redevelopment or road proposals to the office issuing Mortgage Instructions.
6.12.1: Report pre-emption rights or resale restrictions to the office issuing Mortgage Instructions.
6.13.1: Report undischarged improvement/repair grants to the office issuing Mortgage Instructions.
7.3: Obtain written consent to mortgage from all property occupants aged 17 or over.
7.4: Report concerns about information accuracy to the office issuing Mortgage Instructions.
8.1: Legal firms may advise third parties unless a conflict of interest exists.
9.1: Place indemnity insurance policies with the deeds.
9.2: Indemnity insurance limit: 110% of the principal sum.
10.2b: Minimum notice for advance: 5 working days.
10.3: Standard deductions are specified in the Mortgage Offer.
10.7: Return delayed advances via CHAPS within 3 working days.
10.9: Contact the Completions Department at the office issuing Mortgage Instructions for delayed completions.
10.10: Mortgage advances may be held for a maximum of 3 working days.
10.11: Interest on delayed return of funds is charged at the rate quoted in the Mortgage Offer.
12.3.1: Contact Customer Services Dept, PO Box 237, Plymouth, PL1 1WG (DX 133791 Plymouth 13) for retention releases.
14.1.5: Original mortgage deed must be sent with title deeds.
14.2.1: Send title deeds to: Deeds Dept, PO Box 237, Plymouth, PL1 1WG or DX 133791 Plymouth 13.
14.2.2: Post-completion documents include those in Part 1 and the standard deeds schedule.
16.1.1: Contact Customer Services Dept, Plymouth, for title documents.
16.3.7a: Contact Customer Services Dept, Plymouth, for execution of transfer of equity.
16.3.7b: The lender uses a standard form attestation clause.
16.3.1: Lender does not provide a standard form of transfer or deed of covenant.
16.3.2: Contact Customer Services Dept, Plymouth, for debt amounts.
16.3.4: Send the transfer of equity to the lender.
16.4.1: Contact Customer Services Dept, Plymouth, for consent to let applications.

16.5.3a: Send deed of variation to Customer Services Dept, Plymouth.
16.5.3b: Send deed of rectification to Customer Services Dept, Plymouth.
16.5.3c: Send deed of easement to Customer Services Dept, Plymouth.
16.5.3d: Send option agreements to Customer Services Dept, Plymouth.
16.5.2: Draft deeds or agreements must be submitted to Customer Services Dept for consent.
17.1.1: Contact Customer Services Dept, Plymouth, for redemption statements.
17.2.1a: Send discharge and repayment remittance to Customer Services Dept, Plymouth.
